templates.name: Why don't you include assembly guides for your bikes?
templates.content: We don't provide assembly guides with our bikes because we strongly advise professional assembly by a qualified bike shop to ensure everything is adjusted and tightened properly for your safety as a rider. Regardless of your experience as a rider, specialty tools and skills that only a certified bike shop may possess are needed when fully assembling a bike. I really appreciate your patience and understanding on this!

templates.name: What is the difference between c7 and c7i?/ What does the i in the model name mean?
templates.content: If you see an "i" in any of our model name (C7i, M7i, V7i, ect.) that means that bike comes equipped with an internal hub. With an internal gear hub, the gearing system is encased in a sealed mechanism and the chain remains on the same gear at all times. You don't have to fret about the chain falling off, or dirt and grime entering the hub and causing damage. For the average cyclist, a quality internal gear hub lasts a lifetime if properly maintained. The real benefit to an internal hub is that it allows you to shift while coasting, backpedaling, or even at a complete standstill.

templates.name: I'm looking for a 3 speed bike?
templates.content: I'm afraid we do not sell 3-speed bikes.

templates.name: What's an "Effective TT Measurement"?
templates.content: Effective Top Tube (ETT) is a traditional way to measure a bike's length. It measures a horizontal line, parallel to the ground, from the center of the head tube to the intersection with the seat tube or seat post. This number is useful for identifying how long a bike will feel while in a seated position. Hope it helps.

templates.name: Do your bikes have a weight limit?
templates.content: While our bikes do not have an official weight limit, it's safe to assume that the lifespan of some of the components can be significantly reduced for riders over the weight of ~ 275lbs. For riders exceeding this weight, we do generally advise looking into our premium models (internal hubs). They include an upgraded wheel set (double wall) and this is generally the main point of failure. Proper tire pressure will be crucial in protecting the integrity of the wheels and in flat prevention.

templates.name: How long will it take for a partner store to assemble my new bike?
templates.content: Assembly times are dependent on time of year and the service schedule of the partner store, but we find most bikes are typically available for pick within 5-10 business days of delivery. If you need your bike by a certain date, please contact the partner store directly before placing an order to confirm they can accommodate your request.

templates.name: How much do your bikes weigh?
templates.content: Our bikes weigh anywhere from 25-32 lbs, depending on the size and number of gears on the bike. Lighter bikes tend to be made of aluminum or carbon, but we've built our bikes from lightweight steel which will last a lifetime and beyond - and will comfortably absorb the shocks of urban riding significantly better than other materials.

templates.name: How do I start a return?
templates.content: You can start your return here: https://publicbikes.returnscenter.com/

templates.name: Do you sell touch up paint?
templates.content: No, we do not sell touch up paint for previous model years. Most scratches or paint chips do not significantly rust or corrode since they are on the outside of a protective clear coat. If you really the need to touch up scratches we recommend some hobby paint or nail polish. We can supply the PMS color code for many of our colors if you need it.

templates.name: Do assembly partners stock your bike?
templates.content: Assembly partners don't stock our bikes. They can receive bikes ordered online from us and will professionally assemble the bike.

templates.name: Do your bikes come assembled?/What does the unassembled shipping option mean?
templates.content:
If you select our unassembled shipping option, you'll receive an unbuilt bike in a factory box that hasn't been fine-tuned by our mechanics (tools and assembly guide not included). To ensure your bike is safe to ride, we only recommend assembly in a bike shop by professional bicycle mechanics with specialty tools and training.

If you live near a PUBLIC partner store, you can place an order online and pick up your fully assembled PUBLIC bike, zero hassle. If you don't live near one of our partner stores, we recommend our Ready to Ride shipping option where you receive a bike that's been 99% assembled by our expert mechanics and is delivered right to your door!

templates.name: Can I have an item installed on my order?
templates.content: The best way to check on this is to email us directly at customerservice@publicbikes.com or give us a call at 1-888-450-0123. Our team is happy to help!

templates.name: Payment Methods
templates.content: We accept all major credit cards, PayPal, and Affirm.

templates.name: Social media
templates.content: Give us a follow on IG: https://www.instagram.com/publicbikes/ and FB here: https://www.facebook.com/publicbikes/

templates.name: Where are your bikes made / manufactured?
templates.content: We are a California company and we design and spec our bikes in the San Francisco Bay Area. The final assembly and fine-tuning is performed with care by our expert technicians in Marin County, California. The different bike components are sourced from all over the world, from Brooks of England to Shimano of Japan, and the frames are built to our unique specifications by industry leaders in Taiwan.

templates.name: Shipping time
templates.content: It usually takes about 1-2 business days for us to pack up and ship out your order and once shipped, it will take 5-7 business days for accessories and 5-10 business days for bikes.

templates.name: Customer support
templates.content: The best way to handle this is to email us directly at customerservice@publicbikes.com or give us a call at 1-888-450-0123. Our team is happy to help!

templates.name: Cancel / edit order
templates.content: You can change or cancel an order prior to shipment, which is usually within 24 hours of placing the order. Email us at customerservice@publicbikes.com or call our customer service line at 1-888-450-0123 to modify your order if possible.

templates.name: How do I test ride a bike?
templates.content: We have test ride locations in the greater San Francisco Bay Area at all partnering Mike's Bikes locations and other select locations around the country. We also have select Showroom Partners around the country who sell our bikes and can special order a bike for you.

templates.name: Where can I find my bike's serial number?
templates.content: Each of our models features a unique serial number that identifies the make and model of your bike. This also proves ownership of the bike to the purchaser in our customer database. Your unique serial number is engraved into the frame and located underneath the bottom bracket where the crank arms meet the frame.

templates.name: How big are the wheels on the public C1?
templates.content: The C1 comes standard with 700C or roughly about ~ 29".

templates.name: Return policy
templates.content: You can check out our detailed return policy here: https://publicbikes.com/pages/return-policy

templates.name: International shipping
templates.content: At this time, we're only able to ship within the US at the advertised shipping rates. If you have a friend or relative here, we can ship to them, however, you must pick it up and take care of all requisite duty and tax. If you live outside of the continental US and would like to place an order, please contact us directly at 888-450-0123 for an accurate shipping quote.

templates.name: Are adult baskets compatible with kids bikes?
templates.content: No, unfortunately not. Most of our adult baskets aren't compatible with kids bikes.

templates.name: Tracking for In-Store Pickup
templates.content: If you opted for a pickup order, there's no tracking info as the bike isn't technically shipped, it's delivered to the location you chose via our own delivery van. Once the bike has arrived at the store, the associates at the store will call you when it's there and ready.

templates.name: What's the best size for me?
templates.content: A bike that fits properly is more fun to ride! The easiest way to determine the best size for you is by selecting "Find My Size" on the product page of each model.

templates.name: M7i vs C7
templates.content: The M7i offers excellent stand-over clearance between the rider and the top tube of the bike, and its comfortable and sporty design is great for everything from short trips to the farmers market, to speedy cross-town commutes.  The C7 on the other hand offers easy shifting and a wide range of gears that can handle just about any hill around, from San Francisco to Seattle. It's perfect for everyday commuting as well as casual riding, the charming cream commuter tires offer great puncture-resistance and our signature smooth ride over the roughest of city streets.

templates.name: Looking for a road bike that can handle hills
templates.content: You can filter bikes by "Best Use" and "Speed" on the collection pages. Our models that have 9+ speeds are going to more efficient for longer distance riding and hill climbing. Take a look at the models here: https://publicbikes.com/collections/bikes?sort_by=price-descending&filter.p.m.my_fields.speeds=9%2B

templates.name: Store location / retailer
templates.content: You can find us at a store near you here: https://publicbikes.com/pages/partner-stores

templates.name: Can I schedule a date for store pick-up?
templates.content: If you need your bike by a certain date, we recommend contacting the partner store directly before placing an order to confirm they can accommodate your request.

templates.name: Phone support
templates.content: You can give us a call at 1-888-450-0123 Monday-Friday, from 9am to 5pm PST, or let me know if I can help via text, it may be faster.

templates.name: Warranty policy
templates.content: You can take a look at our full warranty policy here: https://publicbikes.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5112739100179-Do-your-bikes-come-with-a-warranty-

templates.name: Military / First responder / Healthcare / Teacher discount
templates.content: Thank you for your service! We don't have any specific discounts like that, but our deals will always be shared with our SMS subscribers or on our website, so keep an eye out!

templates.name: Free shipping
templates.content: We offer free shipping on domestic orders over $75. Maybe you can add something else to reach the minimum?

templates.name: Brand ambassador / sponsorship
templates.content: Thank you for your interest! The best way to discuss this is to email us directly at customerservice@publicbikes.com. Our team is happy to chat.

templates.name: Wholesale inquiries
templates.content: Thank you for your interest! The best way to discuss wholesale is to email us directly at  dealers@publicbikes.com. Our team is happy to chat.

templates.name: How long does my refund take to process?
templates.content: Once we receive and process your return, we will refund the purchase amount to the original form of payment or apply the appropriate credit to an exchange. Depending on your method of return, the entire process - from requesting a return form to receiving a refund - generally takes two to three weeks. You'll receive a confirmation email once your return has been processed (please allow up to 2-4 business days for the refund to show up in your account).

templates.name: Expedited shipping
templates.content: It usually takes about 1-2 business days for us to pack up and ship out your order. Please reach out to customerservice@publicbikes.com if you need an order expedited.

templates.name: Size Chart: All - Clothes
templates.content: You can take a look at our size chart here: https://publicbikes.com/pages/find-your-size

templates.name: Are the handle bars or stem adjustable on the C1?
templates.content: The stem can be raised or lowered a few inches.
